kind: Service

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-public
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-pdb
  namespace: cockroachdb
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      containers:
      - name: cockroachdb
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 26257
          name: grpc
        - containerPort: 8080
          name: http
        command:
        - /cockroach/cockroach
        - start
        - --insecure
        - --advertise-host=$(POD_NAME).crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
        - --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080
        - --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:26257
        - --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257
        env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: COCKROACH_CHANNEL
          value: kubernetes-insecure
        volumeMounts:
        - name: datadir
          mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 2
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF

content.thinking:
So only pod-0 is created, and it's in Running status but not Ready (0/1 ready). The StatefulSet is not creating pod-1 and pod-2. In a StatefulSet, a new pod is only created when the previous pod is "Ready". So pod-0 needs to be Ready before pod-1 is created, and pod-1 needs to be Ready before pod-2 is created.

The issue is that pod-0 is Running but not Ready - probably failing the readiness probe. CockroachDB in `--join` mode won't become healthy until `cockroach init` is run to initialize the cluster. 

This creates a catch-22: the pods need to join together to form a cluster, but the cluster can't be initialized until at least one pod passes its readiness check, and the readiness probe won't pass until the cluster is initialized. I can work around this by either switching to parallel pod startup so all three start at once before running init, or by relaxing the readiness probe requirements during initial bootstrap. Let me look at the pod logs to see what's actually happening.

content.text: Pod-0 is Running but not Ready (readiness probe failing). StatefulSet won't create pod-1 until pod-0 is Ready — the classic CockroachDB init chicken-and-egg. I need `Parallel` pod management so all 3 pods start simultaneously, then run `cockroach init`.

content.thinking:
As expected, pod-0 is waiting for the other nodes to join. With `podManagementPolicy: Parallel`, all 3 pods will start at once, then I can run `cockroach init` once they're all running. The readiness probe currently checks `/health?ready=1` which won't pass until the cluster is initialized. I need to:

1. Patch the StatefulSet to use `podManagementPolicy: Parallel`
2. This will cause pod-1 and pod-2 to start
3. Once all 3 pods are running (but not necessarily ready), run `cockroach init` on pod-0
4. Then the cluster should initialize and pods should become ready

The problem is that `podManagementPolicy` is immutable on existing StatefulSets, so I'll need to delete and recreate it. The core issue is that with the default OrderedReady policy, pod-1 won't start until pod-0 is ready, but pod-0 can't become ready until the cluster is initialized—which requires multiple nodes. Switching to Parallel breaks this deadlock by starting all pods simultaneously.

Since `podManagementPolicy` is immutable, I need to delete the StatefulSet and recreate it with Parallel mode. The good news is the PVCs will persist—Kubernetes doesn't delete them when the StatefulSet is removed. I should also reconsider the readiness probe: the `/health?ready=1` endpoint won't pass until the cluster is initialized, so I might use just `/health` for liveness initially or adjust the readiness criteria. The plan is to delete the StatefulSet, recreate it with Parallel pod management, wait for all three pods to reach Running state, then initialize the cluster.

content.text: Pod-0 is waiting for other nodes. The issue is the default `OrderedReady` policy — each pod waits for the previous to be Ready before starting, but CockroachDB needs all nodes up before any can become Ready. I'll recreate the StatefulSet with `podManagementPolicy: Parallel`.

When I deleted the original StatefulSet and created a new one, pod-0 was reusing the old PVC (datadir-crdb-cluster-0). That PVC had data from when I ran `cockroach init` on the original pod-0. So pod-0 came up as the existing initialized cluster.
Pods 1 and 2 got fresh PVCs with empty data directories, so when they start up they attempt to join the cluster through the join addresses. The problem is that pod-1 can't even connect to itself on localhost:26257 — the node is running but apparently not accepting connections yet, possibly because it's still in the middle of joining. Let me check the recent logs to see what's actually happening.
